How Parole Eligibility is Determined

Most people in prison are eligible for discretionary parole release soon after they enter prison. The parole board has broad discretion to set minimum terms to parole release consideration for each prisoner individually. Time credits against the maximum sentence top out at five days per month (nine percent). Violent offenses are subject to an 85 percent eligibility rule.


In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year

In 2020, there were 155 people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up 14% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years.

Demographics

The demographics of people in prison past their parole consideration year are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old.

Criminal Offenses

In 2020, 55% of people in prison past their parole consideration year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for drugs (26%) and property (22%) offenses.

Original Sentence Lengths

In 2020, most people in prison past their parole consideration year had original sentence lengths between 2 to 4.9 years representing 38% of those eligible for parole.


Missing Data

Prison Population Trends

From 2010 to 2022, the prison population increased 14%, changing from 1,487 in 2010 to 1,689 in 2022.

Source: BJS Prisoners Series


Demographics

The demographics of people in prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old.

Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity


Criminal Offenses

In 2020, 54% of people in prison were incarcerated for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for drugs (24%) and rape or sexual assault (17%) offenses.


Sentence Lengths

In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between 2 to 4.9 years representing 33%.


Prison Release Trends

From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 30%, dropping from 1,009 in 2010 to 1,312 in 2020.


In 2020, 79% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 38% increase compared to 2010.


Conditional Releases

Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions.


Demographics of People Released

The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and other race(s), non-hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community.


Time Served by Offense

Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in North Dakota. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which decreased by 84%.


Incarceration Rates

In North Dakota, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 5.6 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.4 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 5.1 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community.


Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility

  • Race and Ethnicity
  • Gender
In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 267 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole.

Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility

  • Race and Ethnicity
  • Gender

The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date.

In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab.


Time Served

  • Race and Ethnicity
  • Gender

Black people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 1.5 years. Other races people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.1 years.

By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For rape or sexual assault offenses, Black people had 3.3 more years on average compared to Hispanic people, who had the shortest time served for these offenses.
